name: Helium
summary: Day 67 Tuesday. **INDUSTRIAL-GAS-PRODUCER ROTATION THESIS FAILED — LINDE -2.8% TUE (CUMULATIVE -4.2% IN 2 SESSIONS); KORNBLUTH FALSIFICATION HOLDS WEEK 2.** The Apr 30 brief proposed: if Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure persists, industrial-gas producers (Linde, Air Products, Air Liquide) capture spot premiums and outperform semiconductor sector by 5+ percentage points over May 2026. May 1 trial: Linde +1.4% vs SMH +0.6% (initial favorable). May 4-5 verdict: Linde -2.8% Tue, cumulative -4.2% in 2 sessions; SMH -0.6% Tue / +29.2% 1M. Bifurcation INVERTED. Why falsified: spot helium $1,000-1,200/Mcf (doubled since March) — but $-volume capture available to producers is LIMITED. Major chip customers (Micron, Seagate, WD) are on long-term contracts at +20-30% YoY; spot is ~5-10% of helium volume; producer margins are CONTRACTUAL not spot-driven. Linde 2026 guidance not revised UP for helium spot premium. Markets recognize structural insulation. Tier-1 chipmaker falsification HOLDS: Samsung Q1 + SK Hynix sufficient-inventory framings; KOSPI +5.1% Tue record + SK Hynix +12.52% confirms tier-1 chipmakers thriving despite helium cost pressure. Watch alternatives: fab-floor delays at TSMC/Samsung in Q2 earnings (June-July); long-term contract repricing in 2027. Qatar 30-38% global supply still offline. Cape rerouting working. Sadara Chemical JV shutdown unchanged (June 15 grace expiry).

current.price: ~$300+/MCF bulk liquid indicative (Smith, J2 Sourcing); Q2 allocation-notice window pushed to late-May/early-June

current.snapshot_note: TSMC 2-month buffer + Samsung HeRS operational = URGENCY MATERIALLY LOWER than April 14 'two-week clock' priced.
